#eggcurryrecipe #egggravy #eggmasalacurry #roastedeggmasala #roastedeggs #andacurry #eggrecipes #andarecipe #sidedishforrice #sidedishforchapathi #homecookingshow

Prep Time: 5 mins
Cook Time: 30 mins
Servings: 5

Chapters:
Promo - 00:00
Intro - 00:11
How to make Roasted Eggs - 00:37
How to make Egg Curry - 01:23


To Make Roasted Eggs

Egg - 10 Nos
Oil - 1 Tsp
Turmeric Powder - 1/2 Tsp
Red Chilli Powder - 1 Tsp
Black Pepper Powder - 1 Tsp
Salt - 1 Tsp

To Make Egg Curry

Oil - 3 Tbsp
Mustard Seeds - 1/2 Tsp
Cumin Seeds - 1 Tsp
Onion - 3 Nos
Green Chilli - 4 Nos
Ginger Garlic Paste - 2 Tsp
Tomato Puree ( 3 Tomatoes )
Red Chilli Powder - 1 Tsp
Coriander Powder - 1 1/2 Tsp
Salt - 1 Tsp
Pepper Powder - 2 Tsp
Roasted Eggs
Curry Leaves
Coriander Leaves

METHOD:

1. Boil the eggs, remove the shells, cut them into halves and keep them aside.
2. Take a wide, flat pan and add oil. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, black pepper powder and salt.
3. Mix well and saute the powder for 10 seconds. Then place the egg halves and roast them for 2-3 minutes each side on a low flame.
4. Remove the roasted eggs from the pan and keep aside.
5. Take oil in a wide kadai and heat it. Add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Saute.
6. Once the mustard seeds start splattering, add onions and saute until they are golden brown in color.
7. Add green chillies, ginger garlic paste and saute until the raw smell is fully gone.
8. Add the tomato puree and mix. Saute until the moisture is fully gone and the gravy is thickened.
9. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder, black pepper powder and salt. Mix well.
10. Add water, mix well and cook the gravy for 5 minutes.
11. Gently drop the roasted eggs in theh gravy and cook them in the gravy for 5 minutes.
12. Lastly add fresh curry leaves and coriander leaves. Turn off the stove.
13. Yummy, tangy and spicy egg curry is ready to be served hot and nice with rice/roti.

Egg curry is a favorite dish for many people. It can be done in many ways. In this video, you can see a very easy gravy based egg curry which can be done with regular ingredients available in our kitchens all the time. This is a South Indian style, Tamilian style dish, to be precise, which is a bit tangy as well as spicy. You can adjust the spice according to your taste. You can enjoy this for rice/roti. It can be served at lunches or dinners. You can also take this for lunch boxes. Watch this video to get a step by step guidance on how to make this egg curry easily at home. Do try this recipe and enjoy.

title: Anda Masala Curry Dhaba Style Recipe | ढाबे वाली अंडा मसाला करी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
description:
Full written recipe for Dhaba style egg curry

Prep time: 15-20 minutes
Cooking time: 25-30 minutes
Serves: 3-4 people

Ingredients:
Fried eggs:
• Ghee 1 tbsp
• Boiled eggs 8 nos.
• Kashmiri red chilli powder a pinch
• Haldi powder a pinch
• Salt to taste
For curry:
• Ghee 2 tbsp + oil 1 tbsp
• Whole spices:
1. Jeera 1 tsp
2. Dalchini 1 inch
3. Green cardamom 2-3 pods
4. Black cardamom 1 no.
5. Tej patta 1 no.
• Onions 5 medium size / 400 gm (chopped)
• Ginger garlic chilli ½ cup (roughly chopped)
• Powdered spices:
1. Turmeric powder ½ tsp
2. Spicy red chilli powder 2 tsp
3. Kashmiri red chilli powder 1 tbsp
4. Coriander powder 2 tbsp
5. Jeera powder 1 tsp
• Tomatoes 4 medium size (chopped)
• Salt to taste
• Garam masala 1 tsp
• Kasuri methi 1 tsp
• Ginger 1 inch (julienned)
• Green chillies 2-3 nos. (slit)
• Fresh coriander a small handful
Method:
• Set a pan on medium heat, add ghee, boiled eggs, red chilli powder, haldi & salt, stir & shallow fry the eggs for couple of minutes.` keep the shallow fried eggs aside to be used later.
• For curry, set a wok on medium heat, add ghee & the whole spices, stir & further add chopped onions, stir and cook until the onions turn golden brown in colour.
• Add the roughly chopped ginger garlic chilli, stir & cook for 3-4 minutes on medium flame.
• Further lower the flame and add the powdered spices, mix well & add some hot water to avoid the spices from burning.
• Increase the flame to medium heat, stir & cook until the ghee is released.
• Now, add the tomatoes & salt, stir & cook well for at least 8-10 minutes or until the tomatoes has mixed well with the masala.
• Add some hot water, stir & cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
• Now, add the shallow fried eggs, stir & cook on high flame for 5-6 minutes.
• Now add ginger, green chillies, kasuri methi, garam masala & freshly chopped coriander leaves, stir well. 
• You can adjust the consistency of the gravy by adding hot water as required, your dhaba style egg curry is ready, serve hot with some tandoori roti or any Indian bread of your choice.

Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार 
2 tsp Vinegar, सिरका
7-8 Eggs, अंडे
Chilled water, ठंडा पानी

For Gravy
1 ½ tbsp Oil, तेल
2 Garlic cloves, roughly sliced, लहसुन
1 inch Ginger, peeled, roughly sliced, अदरक
3 medium Onions, roughly chopped, प्याज 
1 tbsp Degi red chili powder, देगी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 
2 medium size Tomato, roughly chopped, टमाटर 
Little water, पानी
Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार 
1 heaped tbsp Coriander powder, धनिया पाउडर

For Finishing Gravy
¼ cup Water, पानी
Prepared Gravy, तैयार की हुई ग्रेवी
1 tbsp Coriander leaves, chopped, धनिया पत्ता
Fried Eggs, तले हुए अंडे

For Frying Eggs 
2 tsp Oil, तेल
7-8 hard-boiled Eggs, अंडे
Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार 
1 tsp Degi red chili powder, देगी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 

For Garnish
Coriander sprig, धनिया पत्ता

Process 
For Boiling Eggs 
In a sauce pot, add water as required, vinegar, eggs and give it a boil.
Cook it for 10-12 minutes or until hard boiled. Transfer it to chilled water and remove the shell.
Keep it aside for further use.

For Gravy 
In a heavy bottom pan or a kadai, add oil, once it's hot, add garlic, ginger, onions and saute for 3-4 minutes or until the onions are translucent.
Add degi red chili powder, tomatoes, salt to taste and mix well. 
Add some water, salt to taste, coriander powder and saute for a minute.
Cover it with the lid and cook until the tomatoes are mushy.
Transfer it to a grinder jar and grind it into a smooth paste.

For Finishing Gravy 
In the same kadai, add water, prepared gravy and let it simmer.
Add coriander leaves, prepared fried eggs and cook for 4-5 minutes or until the gravy thickens.
Transfer it to a serving dish and garnish it with coriander sprig.
Serve hot with roti or rice. 

For Frying Eggs 
In a pan, add oil, once it’s hot, add hard boiled eggs and toss it.
Add salt to taste, degi red chili powder and toss well. 
Turn off the flames and keep it aside for further use.

title: AFGHANI EGG CURRY | AFGHANI EGG MASALA | AFGHANI EGG GRAVY RECIPE
description:
Afghani Egg Curry | Afghani Egg Gravy Recipe | Afghani Anda Curry | Afghani Egg Korma | Afghani Egg Recipe | Afghani Anda Gravy Recipe | Anda Curry | Egg Curry | Egg Gravy | Egg Curry Recipe

Shop CELA Brand of Kitchen Products by Spice Eats - https://amzn.to/4rx93bX

Ingredients for Afghani Egg Curry:

(Tsp-Teaspoon; Tbsp-Tablespoon) 

Boiled Eggs-6 nos.

For frying eggs-
- Oil- 1 tbsp
- Turmeric powder-1/2 tsp
- Kashmiri Chilli Powder-1/2 tsp

For the Masala Paste:
- Onions sliced- 2 medium (150 gms)
- Ginger chopped - 1” (8-10 gms)
- Garlic cloves-5-6 
- Green chillies - 4 (12 gms)
- Cashew nuts-10 
- Coriander leaves-handful (10 gms)

Tempering:
- Green Cardamom-3
- Cloves- 3
- Cinnamon-2 

Spice Powders:
- Turmeric powder- pinch 
- Cumin powder-1/2 tsp
- Black pepper powder-1/2 tsp
- Garam Masala powder-1/2 tsp
- Roasted & crushed Kasuri Methi- 1/2 tsp

Other Ingredients:
- Whisked Curd/ Yogurt- 3 tbsp
- Fresh cream- 2-3 tbsp
- Oil- 3 tbsp
- Salt- 3/4 tsp
- Coriander leaves, chopped for garnish- 2 tbsp 


Preparation:

- To make the Masala paste, heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan and add the sliced onions. Fry on medium heat for around 3-4 mins and then add the chopped ginger, garlic, green chillies and the cashew nuts.
- Mix and fry on medium heat for around 1-2 mins.
- Remove onto a plate and cool it.
- To make the paste, add this mixture into the grinder and coarse grind it. 
- Now add 1/2 cup water and make it into a smooth paste. 
- Shell the boiled eggs and make 3-4 cuts on one side. Set aside. 

Process:

- Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan and add 1/2 tsp each of turmeric and Kashmiri chilli powder. 
- Give a stir and add the cut boiled eggs.
- Stir and fry on low heat for around 2 mins till the eggs are golden brown in colour. Remove the fried eggs. 
- Now add 1-2 tbsp oil in the same oil left in the pan .
- Heat the oil and then add the whole spices.
- Give a stir and then add the Masala paste made earlier in the grinder.
- Cook on low heat for 3-4 mins and then add the spice powders and salt and cook on low heat for another 3-4 mins. 
- Now add the fresh cream and whisked curd mix and cook on low heat for 2-3 mins till oil separates.
- You can switch off the heat before you add the curd and then switch on after giving a stir. 
- Now add the crushed kasuri methi, mix and simmer on low heat for 2-3 mins till oil separates.
- Add 150 ml water and then add the fried eggs, mix lightly and cover & cook on low heat for 5 mins.
- Remove lid and add the chopped coriander leaves and little water if required. Simmer for 1-2 mins and serve hot.

title: Egg Curry Recipe | Egg Masala Curry Recipe | Egg Recipes | Easy & Quick Recipe | Varun Inamdar
description:
Learn How To Cook Egg Masala Curry recipe from Chef Varun Inamdar. Make this authentic egg recipe in a simple, quick and easy method at your home and share your experience with us in the comments below.

Ingredients:-
6 Hard Boiled Eggs, peeled
1/4 tsp turmeric powder
1 tbsp oil

For the Gravy
2 tbsp oil
1 Bay Leaf
1 Cinnamon stick
2 nos green cardamom
1 no. Black cardamom
4 nos Cloves
2 tbsp Butter
200 gms boiled onion paste
200 gms fresh tomato puree
1tbsp ginger garlic crushed
2 tbsp Red Chilli powder
1 tsp Garam Masala
1/2 tsp Turmeric
1 tsp Kasoori Methi, crushed
Salt to taste
Fresh corriander leaves


Method:-
In a pan heat oil and add black cardamom, green cardamom, cloves, bay leaves and cinnamon stick. Once this starts crackling add in the boiled onion paste and give a quick mix. Add salt, ginger-garlic paste and let it cook for a minute on high flame or until golden brown in colour. Then add the turmeric powder, red chilli powder, garam masala powder, butter and cook till the raw flavour of spices goes away. Once the masala is cooked completely, add in the tomato puree, salt and cook on high flame for 2-3 minutes. Once the oil leaves the sides of the pan, add water as required and cook for 10 minutes with the lid on. 
To pan fry the hard boiled eggs, add some oil to a pan, turmeric powder and then the boiled eggs and fry the eggs on high flame until golden brown on all sides. Once the eggs are fried & ready add them to the gravy and cook it for 30-40 seconds.
Add water as and if required.
Egg Masala Curry is ready to eat!

To make Egg Lababdar

Egg - 6 Nos
Tomato - 4 Nos
Cashew Nuts - 20 Nos
Oil - 4 Tbsp
Whole Spices
( Bay Leaf, Cinnamon, Cardamom, Cloves )
Cumin Seeds -1 Tsp
Onion - 2 Nos
Ginger Garlic Paste - 1 Tsp
Turmeric Powder - 1/4 Tsp
Kashmiri Chilli - 2 Tsp
Cumin Powder - 1 Tsp
Coriander Powder - 1 Tsp
Salt - 1 Tsp
Water - 1 Cup
Green Chilli - 4 Nos
Garam Masala- 1 Tsp
Sugar - 1 Tsp
Kasuri Methi
Coriander Leaves 

Method

To a mixer jar, take four tomatoes, 20 soaked cashewnuts and grind it nicely. Tranfer the puree to a bowl and keep it aside.
To a wide kadai, add 4tbsp of oil, add bayleaf, cinnamon, cardamom and cloves.
Then add chopped onions, and cook it to a golden brown colour.
Add ginger garlic paste and mix well.
Add tomato puree and little water and cook well.
Once it is cooked, add turmeric powder, Kashmiri chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, salt, and mix well.
Add one cup of water, slit four green chillies, garam masala powder, sugar and allow it to cook for five minutes.
Add crushed kasoori methi and mix.
Remove the bay leaf.
Crack open the eggs and pour it in the mixture, sprinkle the coriander leaves  and allow it to cook for ten minutes.
Egg lababdar is done.
Serve it nice and hot with Roti or Rice of your choice.

title: Jammy Egg Curry | Rich Indian Egg Curry with Whole Spices
description:
Ingredients:
•6 eggs (jammy boiled- see method below)
•Oil, for browning onions and using that leftover oil for cooking as well
•400 g onions, sliced
•8 tbsp full-fat yogurt
•20 g garlic
•15 g ginger
•½ tsp black peppercorns
•2 bay leaves
•4 cloves
•5 green cardamoms
•1 mace blade
•Cinnamon 3-4cm long
•1.5 tsps Kashmiri red chilli powder
•2 cups warm water
•Salt, to taste
•Fresh coriander + green chillies, for garnish

⸻

Method
~ To make jammy eggs: bring a pot of water to a rolling boil. Gently lower in the eggs and boil for 8 minutes. Transfer immediately into iced water, then peel.

~ Heat oil in a pot and fry sliced onions until just browned at the edges. Sprinkle a little salt. Remove and set aside and once cooled, blend it with yogurt.

~ In the same oil, add bay leaves, cloves, cardamom, mace, cinnamon and black pepper. Let them sizzle and release aroma.

~ Add ginger–garlic paste, stir until lightly golden.

~ Add the onions and yoghurt paste to the pot along with Kashmiri red chilli powder + salt. Cook until the colour deepens from pale red to a rich reddish-brown and oil begins to separate.

~ Stir in 2 cups warm water. Bring to a gentle boil.

~ Add the jammy eggs (reserve a couple to slice for garnish). Simmer briefly so the flavours soak in.

~ Garnish with coriander, green chillies and the sliced eggs on top. Serve hot with rotis or rice.

Prep Time: 10 mins
Cook Time: 40 mins
Servings: 4-5

Ingredients

To Make Masala Paste
Onion - 2 Nos Chopped
Green Chilli - 6 Nos Chopped
Chopped Ginger
Garlic
Coriander Leaves
Mint Leaves

To Make Egg Malai Curry
Eggs - 8 Nos
Oil - 3 Tbsp
Whole Spices
(Cinnamon, Clove & Cardamom)
Shahi Jeera - 1 Tsp
Ground Masala Paste
Turmeric Powder - 1/2 Tsp
Coriander Powder - 1 Tsp
Cumin Powder - 1 Tsp
Garam Masala - 1 Tsp
Salt
Curd - 100 Ml
Water - 1 Cup
Sugar - 1 Tsp
Chopped Coriander Leaves
Fresh Cream - 1/4 cup

Method:

1. To make the masala paste, add onions, green chillies, ginger, garlic cloves, coriander leaves, mint leaves to a mixer jar and grind into a fine paste. Keep it aside.
2. Take water in a pot and boil. Add eggs and salt. Boil the eggs and remove the shells. Keep them aside.
3. To a pan, add oil and put the whole spices and shahi jeera. Saute.
4. Now add the ground masala paste, mix and cover the pan. Cook for 10 mins.
5. After 10 mins, add turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder, salt and mix well.
6. Add in the smoothly whisked fresh curd, mix and cook for 5 mins.
7. Pour water into the gravy and simmer for 10 mins by covering with a lid.
8. Add sugar, mix and add the slit boiled eggs.
9. Mix well, add chopped coriander leaves and cover the pan. Simmer for 5 mins.
10. Add fresh cream and mix well.
11. Creamy, rich and delicious Hyderabadi Egg Malai Curry is ready to be served hot.

description:

It’s perfect for bachelors, hostel students, and new home cooks who want something comforting and delicious. Do try it, I know you’ll love it. Happy cooking! ❤️

Full written recipe - Home-Style Egg Curry
Prep time: 15–20 minutes
Cooking time: 35–40 minutes
Serves: 3–4

Ingredients:
Vegetable Prep
ONION | प्याज़ 3 MEDIUM SIZED
TOMATO | टमाटर 2 MEDIUM SIZED
First Cooking (Base Masala)
OIL | तेल 1–2 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH
GARLIC | लहसुन A HANDFUL (20 CLOVES)
GINGER | अदरक 3–4 INCH
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 2 NOS.
FRESH CORIANDER STEMS | हरा धनिया डंठल A HANDFUL
SALT | नमक A PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
WATER | पानी AS REQUIRED
Final Cooking of Gravy
GHEE | घी 1 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
BAY LEAF | तेज पत्ता 2 NOS.
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 3 NOS.
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1 INCH
BLACK CARDAMOM | बड़ी इलायची 1 NO.
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा A LARGE PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/2 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TBSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 2 TBSP
SPICY RED CHILLI POWDER | तीखी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED

Eggs
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
BOILED EGGS | उबले अंडे 6 NOS.
SALT | नमक A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर A PINCH
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर A PINCH
Final Seasoning
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED
Method:
Take onions and roughly slice them. You can also use a slicer or chop them using a chopper.
Remove the eye from the tomatoes and slice them into thick slices.
Heat a vessel on high flame and add oil. Once the oil is hot, add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add the sliced onions and roast on high flame, stirring continuously, until they turn golden brown and caramelized. In between, add splashes of hot water to help the onions caramelize faster without burning.
Once the onions are well browned, add garlic, ginger, green chillies, fresh coriander stems, sliced tomatoes, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2–3 minutes on high flame.
Add hot water as required, cover, and cook on low flame for 10–15 minutes until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy.
After 10–15 minutes, uncover and switch off the flame. Let the mixture cool down completely.
Once cooled, transfer the mixture to a mixer jar and grind into a fine puree using water as required. Keep the puree aside.
Heat a kadhai on high flame and add ghee and oil. Once hot, add bay leaf, green cardamom, cinnamon, black cardamom, and cumin seeds. Let the whole spices crackle well.
Add the prepared puree to the kadhai. Immediately add turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and spicy red chilli powder. Mix well.
Cook the masala on medium-high flame for 7–8 minutes, stirring continuously, until the oil separates and the gravy starts leaving the sides of the kadhai.
Adjust the consistency by adding hot water as required. The gravy should be smooth, velvety, and medium-thick, not too thin and not too thick.
Cover the kadhai, reduce the flame to low, and let the gravy simmer.
For tempering the boiled eggs, heat a non-stick pan and add oil. Once hot, add the boiled eggs along with salt, turmeric powder, and Kashmiri red chilli powder. Toss the eggs gently so they get evenly coated and lightly roasted for better texture.
Remove the eggs into a bowl and keep aside.
Check the gravy consistency and ensure the oil has released on top.
Add the tempered eggs to the gravy. Add roasted kasuri methi powder and garam masala. Mix gently.
Taste and adjust salt if required. Add chopped fresh coriander and mix well.
Your delicious, smooth and velvety Egg Curry is ready. You can enjoy it with some tandoori rotis, chapati and rice.

title: Egg Masala Recipe | Simple Egg Curry Recipe | Egg Recipes | How To Make Egg Masala | Smita
description:
Download the Get Curried App by clicking on this link:- http://bit.ly/GetCurriedVegNonVegRecipes_And

Learn How To Make Egg Masala Recipe from Chef Smita Deo only on Get Curried. Make this Simple Egg Curry Recipe, a quick and easy method of an Egg Recipe at your home and share your experience with us in the comments below. 

Ingredients:-
5 Eggs, boiled
2 tbsp. Ghee
½ tsp. Kasuri methi powder
1 tsp. Each - Ginger, garlic and green chilli paste
3 onions fried golden and ground to a paste
3 tomatoes boiled and pureed
1 tsp. Chilli powder
½ tsp. Turmeric powder
1 tbsp. Coriander powder
1 tsp. Cumin seed powder
4 tbsp. Cashew nuts ground to a fine paste
¼ cup Cream
Salt to taste
2 medium potatoes halved and deep fried till golden and done
Fresh chopped coriander for garnishing


Method:-
1. Heat ghee and add the kasuri methi powder and the ginger, garlic and green chilli paste.
2. Add fried onion paste and the tomatoes and sauté for 4-5 minutes.
3. Add chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander and cumin seed powder and fry until the oil oozes out.
4. Add cashew paste and cream and sauté further for 1 minute.
5. Add 1 cup of water and salt and make a thick gravy.
6. Add boiled eggs and potatoes and cover and cook for 5 minutes on a low flame.
7. Garnish with coriander and serve along with laccha paratha.

Egg Masala is ready to eat!

description:
Full written recipe for Egg Butter Masala

Prep time: 15-20 minutes
Cooking time: 25-30 minutes
Serves: 4-5 people

Ingredients:
Tomato Puree
TOMATO | टमाटर 4 NOS. (ROUGHLY CHOPPED)
GARLIC | लेहसुन 8-10 CLOVES
GINGER | अदरक 1 INCH
CORIANDER STEMS | धनिए के डंठल 2 TBSP
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1 NO. 
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर 1.5 TBSP
CORIANDER POWDER | धनिया पाउडर 1 TBSP
CUMIN POWDER | जीरा पाउडर 1/2 TSP
CASHEW | काजू 15 NOS. (SOAKED)
Eggs
BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP
OIL | तेल 1 TSP
EGG | अंडे 6 NOS. (BOILED)
SALT | नमक A PINCH
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर A PINCH
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर A PINCH
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A SMALL HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
Final gravy
BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP 
OIL | तेल 1 TBSP
CUMIN SEEDS | जीरा 1/2 TSP 
CINNAMON | दालचीनी 1/2 INCH
GREEN CARDAMOM | हरी इलायची 2 NOS. 
GREEN CHILLI | हरी मिर्च 1-2 NOS. (CHOPPED)
GARLIC | लेहसुन 2 TBSP (CHOPPED)
GINGER | अदरक 1/2 INCH (CHOPPED)
ONION | प्याज़ 3 NOS. (CHOPPED)
TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर 1/4 TSP
KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च 1/2 TSP
HOT WATER | गरम पानी A SPLASH 
SALT | नमक TO TASTE
SUGAR | शक्कर A PINCH
HOT WATER | गरम पानी AS REQUIRED
FRESH CREAM | फ्रेश क्रीम 2-3 TBSP
BUTTER | मक्खन 1 TBSP
FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED)
ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी हुई कसूरी मेथी का पाउडर A PINCH
GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH
Method:
In a mixer grinder jar add tomato, garlic, ginger & all the remaining ingredients of the tomato puree & grind it into a fine puree, your base puree for the gravy is ready.
To saute the eggs set a pan over high flame & once it gets hot, add in the butter & oil.
Add the boiled eggs along with salt, turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder & fresh coriander, stir well & saute the eggs until a thin brown layer forms on the eggs the transfer them into a bowl & set aside.
To make the gravy set a deep pan over high heat & once it gets hot, add in the butter & oil & let it get hot.
Further add cumin seeds, cinnamon, green cardamom, green chilli, garlic & ginger, stir well & cook over medium high flame for a minute.
Add chopped onions, stir well & cook over medium high flame until they turn golden brown.
Once the onions turn golden brown, lower the flame & add turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder & a splash of hot water, stir & increase the flame to medium high & cook the spices for 1-2 minutes.
Further add the prepared tomato puree followed by salt & sugar, stir well & cook the gravy over medium flame until it starts leaving the surface of the pan, this process will take upto 15-20 minutes.
Once you have cooked the gravy for 15-20 minutes & it stops sticking to the pan add hot water to adjust the consistency & stir well.
Further add the sauteed eggs, fresh cream, butter, fresh coriander, roasted kasuri methi powder, garam masala, stir well & cook for 1-2 minutes then taste & adjust salt if required.
Your delicious egg butter masala is ready.

title: Village Style Egg Curry | Rich & Spicy Egg Curry Recipe
description:
Ingredients
Eggs-5 nos
Onion - 3 to 4 nos
Tomatoes - 2 nos
Garlic -5 to 6 nos
Ginger - 1 nos
Green chilli - 2 to 3 nos
Curry leaves - 2 to 3 sprigs
Bay leaves - 1 small
Fennel seed - 1 tsp
Star anise-1 nos
Cinnamon- small pieces
Red chilli powder - 11/2 tsp
Turmeric powder - 1/2 tsp
Pepper powder - 1 tsp
Garam masala - 1 tsp
Coriander powder - 1 tsp
Oil - 2 tsp
Salt -1 or 2 tsp

Method
Heat water in a pan, add salt and eggs.
Cover and boil them well.
Then peel the onion, ginger and garlic.
Then wash the onion, ginger and garlic, set aside.
Then chopped the onion and set aside.
Then chopped the tomatoes and green chilli and set aside.
Then cut the ginger into small pieces and set aside.
Then crush the garlic and ginger, set aside.
Once the egg is boiling, remove from the fire and peel and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan and add Bay leaves and fennel seeds.
Then add the chopped onion and salt and sauté it well.
Add curry powder such as red chilli powder, garam masala and turmeric powder.
Then add coriander powder and pepper powder.
Saute till the curry powders raw smell completely gone.
Then add the chopped tomatoes and sauté them well.
Pour a sufficient amount of water and mix it well.
Add some curry leaves and cook them well till the curry masala is well cooked.
Cover and cook them well.
Remove the lid and add the boiled eggs and cook for a few minutes.
Remove from fire and set aside.
Serve the tasty Egg Curry with chapati or appam.

title: Kerala Egg Curry With Coconut Milk | Recipe For Simple Egg Curry | Easy 30 Minute Weekday Dinners
description:
For a detailed recipe - click below
***👇👇👇👇***
https://www.kannammacooks.com/egg-curry-coconut-milk/

Here are some of the things you can buy online for making this curry
Hard Anodized Small Tadka Pan https://amzn.to/3NxjVSN
Earthen Kadai/Clay Pot https://amzn.to/35H6dfF
Teak-wood Chopping Board https://amzn.to/3hZa4qm
Carote Knife https://amzn.to/3IWGrnd
Glass Mixing Bowls (500 ml) https://amzn.to/3hMJnVD

Recipe

Main Ingredients for the Egg Curry

2 tablespoon coconut oil
1 small piece cinnamon
2 cardamom
3 cloves
1/2 teaspoon fennel seeds
1 sprig curry leaves
1/2 inch ginger, julienne
2 dried red chillies
3 green chillies
4 cloves garlic, sliced
1/2 cup onion, sliced
2 tomatoes, sliced
1 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon turmeric powder
1/2 teaspoon red chilli powder
1/2 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder
1/2 teaspoon black pepper powder
1/4 teaspoon garam masala powder
1 tablespoon coriander powder
1 cup water
1/2 teaspoon sugar
4 hard boiled eggs
250 ml canned coconut milk
1/2 tomato, deseeded and sliced

For Tempering the Egg Curry

1 tablespoon coconut oil
2 tablespoon Indian shallots, sliced
1 sprig curry leaves
1/2 teaspoon black pepper powder

Method

Add coconut oil to a kadai. Let it heat up. Add in a small piece of cinnamon. Add a couple of cardamom pieces. Add the cloves. Add some fennel seeds. Add a sprig of fresh curry leaves. Add in some finely julienned ginger. Add a couple of dried red chillies. Saute for a few seconds. Add in the green chillies, sliced garlic and sliced onions. Saute the onions for a few minutes till they are soft. Add in a couple of tomatoes that have been sliced. Add some salt at this stage so the tomatoes cook faster. Mix everything well to combine. Cover the pan with a lid and cook on a low flame till the tomatoes are mushy.

Once the tomatoes are mushy, add in the turmeric powder, red chilli powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder - (Kashmiri red chilli powder is mainly for the color and is not spicy), black pepper powder, garam masala powder and coriander powder. Saute for a few seconds. Add a cup of water to the pan. Add a little sugar for balance and mix everything well to combine. Cover the pan with a lid and cook on a low flame for about five minutes.

In the meantime, get the hard boiled eggs ready. Make some gashes on the eggs for the flavours of the curry to penetrate into the eggs.  

Add the prepared eggs to the pan. Mix well to combine. Add about 250 ml of coconut milk to the pan. Canned coconut milk works perfectly fine for this recipe. Mix well. Do not cook for long after adding the coconut milk. At this stage add some tomato slices that have been deseeded and sliced. Let the curry come to a mere simmer.

In the meantime, let's prepare a tempering.
Heat coconut oil in a small tempering kadai. Add in the sliced Indian shallots and the curry leaves. Saute for a few minutes till the shallots are roasted golden. Once the shallots are golden, switch off the flame and add in the black pepper powder. Mix well. Add the tempering to the egg curry.

Our delicious egg curry is now ready. Enjoy with appam, idiyappam, dosai and this curry goes well with rice too.

title: DHABA SPECIAL EGG CURRY | DHABA STYLE EGG MASALA | EGG MASALA CURRY
description:
Dhaba Special Egg Curry | Dhaba Style Egg Masala | Egg Masala Curry | Dhaba Style Anda Masala Recipe | Dhaba Style Egg Masala | Anda Curry Dhaba Style | Egg Masala Curry | Egg Masala Gravy | Egg Curry by Spice Eats


Ingredients for Dhaba Special Egg Curry:

- Boiled Eggs- 6 

For frying eggs:
- Oil- 1 tbsp
- Turmeric powder-1/4 tsp
- Red Chilli Powder- 1/4 tsp 

Tempering:
1. Cumin seeds- 1 tsp
2. Whole red chillies- 2 

Spice Powders:
- Red Chilli Powder- 1 tsp
- Coriander powder- 1 tsp
- Cumin powder- 1/2 tsp
- Kashmiri Chilli Powder- 1/2 tsp
- Garam Masala Powder- 3/4 tsp
- Kasuri Methi (dry roasted & powdered)- 1 tsp

Other Ingredients:
- Onions, fine chopped- 2 medium (150 gms)
- Ginger, fine chopped- 2 tsp
- Garlic, fine chopped- 2 tsp
- Green chillies, chopped- 2 nos
- Fresh Tomato paste- 3 medium (200 gms)
- Besan- 2 tsp to be mixed in 2 tbsp of water
- Oil- 3 tbsp
- Butter- 1 tbsp
- Coriander leaves, chopped- 3-4 tbsp
- Salt- 1 tsp

Preparation:

- Boil the eggs & shell them. Now heat oil in a pan, add 1/4th tsp turmeric powder , 1/4 tsp Chilli powder & give a stir.
- Now add the boiled eggs and stir & fry on low heat for 1-2 mins till golden in colour. Remove and set aside. 
- Fine chop the onions, ginger, garlic, green chillies & coriander leaves.
- Blend red & ripe tomatoes into a paste
- Dry roast the Kasuri methi for 30 secs. Cool and crush it into a coarse powder. 

Process:

- Heat 3 tbsp of oil in a kadhai. Add the cumin seeds & allow it to splutter.
- Now add the red chillies and give a stir.
- Now add the chopped onions and fry on medium high to medium low heat for around 8 mins till the onions are light brown in colour. Also add a pinch of salt after adding the onions.
- Add in the chopped ginger, garlic & green chilies. Fry for another 2 mins on low heat.
- Now add the tomato paste, mix well & then add 1 tsp of salt.
- Fry/bhunno for around 5-6 mins till the tomato is totally cooked, gravy is like a smooth sauce & oil separates.
- Now add the Chilli powder,coriander powder & cumin powder. 
- Fry for another 2-3 mins till the masala is cooked.
- Now add the besan mixed in water & fry on low heat stirring continuously for 2-3 mins till the besan is cooked.
- Add 200 ml water and then add the Kashmiri chilli powder, garam masala powder & Kasuri Methi. Mix well & cook for a minute.
- Drop in the fried eggs, mix well, cover & cook for around 5 mins.
- Garnish with coriander leaves & mix well.
- Add a tbsp of butter to finish.
- Serve hot with roti or rice.

title: EGG MALAI MASALA | EGG MALAI CURRY | EGG MASALA CURRY | EGG CURRY
description:
Egg Malai Curry or Egg Malai Masala is a low spice Egg Curry cooked in a creamy gravy without any cream! This unique Egg Curry  was first made by Spice Eats and shared on YouTube. An easy to cook recipe that’s tasty as well as healthy. 

***********************
For Vegetarian Recipes follow our sister channel @SpiceSwad 
***********************

Ingredients: 

Eggs- 4 (boiled, shelled, cut in halves) 

Onions - 2 medium, roughly cubed
Green chillies - 2
Ginger-garlic paste - 1 tsp 
Cumin (Zeera) powder - 1 tsp
Pepper powder - 1/2 tsp
Garam Masala powder- 1 tsp (Link to our Garam Masala Recipe: https://youtu.be/1trtejl8hwM)
Coriander leaves - 1 bunch ( finely chopped)
Milk - 200 ML
Salt - 1 tsp or to taste

Preparation: 

- Boil the eggs & cut into halves 
- Put the cubed onions & green chillies into a grinder & blend into a smooth paste
- Fine chop a bunch of coriander leaves 

Process : 

- In a kadhai, add oil & once medium hot , add the onion-green chilli paste. Lower flame & cook for 2 mins 
- Now add the ginger-garlic paste & mix well 
- Keep cooking in low flame , stirring often, for around 15 mins till paste changes colour 
- Now switch off the flame & add the milk stirring continuously. Mix it well with the Masala 
- Switch on the flame & keep in low flame till oil separates
- Put in the cumin powder, pepper powder, garam masala powder & salt . Mix well & cook for a few mins 
- Place the egg halves in the gravy, yolk side down to absorb the spices. 
- Cover & cook for few mins
- After few mins turn the egg halves yolk side up. 
- Add the finely chopped coriander leaves, mix well
- Cook covered for a few mins till oil separates 
- Serve, enjoy with rice or roti

EGG CURRY
Preparation time: 15 minutes
Cooking time: 30 minutes
Serves: 4

Ingredients
2 tbsp Oil
1 tbsp Ghee
1 black cardamom
1 Cinnamon stick
1 bay leave
1 cloves
1 tsp Cumin seeds
4 Onion, chopped
1 tsp Ginger garlic paste
½ tsp turmeric powder
1 tsp red chilli powder
1 tsp coriander powder
¼ tsp kasuri methi , crushed
4 tomato, chopped
1 no egg yolk, boiled
Salt to taste
4 nos eggs, boiled
1 tsp oil
½ tsp red chilli powder
½ tsp turmeric powder

For omelet:-
4 no Egg
Salt to taste
¼ tsp red chilli powder
¼ tsp turmeric powder
½ tsp Oil
1 tbsp coriander, chopped
For garnish
2-3 coriander spring

Process:-
● In a kadai heat oil, ghee. Add black cardamom, cinnamon stick, bay leave, cloves and cumin
seeds saute well.
● Add onion and saute till it become translucent. Add ginger garlic paste, turmeric powder,
red chilli powder, coriander powder and saute well add kasuri methi, tomato and saute well
till the masala gets cooked.
● Add ¼ cup water and let it cooked, add 1 egg yolk boiled so the gravy gets thicken. Add salt
to taste and let it cook.
● In a pan, Add oil, boiled and pricked egg , red chilli powder, turmeric powder and saute till
colour appear an keep it aside.
● For omelet, In a mixing bowl add eggs, turmeric powder, red chilli powder and salt to taste
and mix them well.
● In another pan add oil pour the egg mixture and cook the omelette soft in texture.
● Garnish with coriander
● Serve the gravy in two form, one with boiled egg cut into half and serve hot and one with omelette cut into half.

title: Dum Ke Ande | Unique Egg Curry Recipe | Creamy Egg Curry
description:
Welcome to Osama Jalali’s Food World ❤️

Today we are making a unique and flavourful dish — Dum Ke Ande.

Unlike regular egg curry, this recipe cooks raw eggs directly in a rich, creamy masala gravy. The eggs gently cook on dum, absorbing all the flavours of spices and kaju-based curry, making it soft, rich and delicious.

Perfect for a quick meal, this recipe is simple yet gives a restaurant-style taste at home.

🥣 Ingredients

• Oil – 100 ml
• Cumin (zeera) – 1 tsp
• Black cardamom – 2
• Bay leaf – 2
• Onions – 2 (chopped)
• Ginger garlic paste – 1 tbsp
• Tomato puree – 4 tomatoes
• Salt – to taste
• Turmeric – 1 pinch
• Red chilli powder – 1 tbsp
• Coriander powder – 1 tbsp
• Cumin powder – 1 tsp
• Cashew paste – from 15 cashews
• Garam masala – 1 tsp
• Eggs – 4 (raw)

Garnish

• Fresh coriander
• Green chillies

👨‍🍳 Method

Heat oil in a kadhai and add cumin, black cardamom and bay leaf.

Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.

Add ginger garlic paste and tomato puree. Cook for 5 minutes.

Add salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder and cumin powder.
Cook well until the masala releases oil.

Add cashew paste and mix thoroughly.
Add garam masala and adjust the consistency with water.

Now gently crack the raw eggs directly into the gravy.

Cover with a lid and cook on dum for about 10 minutes until eggs are set.

Garnish with fresh coriander and green chillies.

Serve hot with roti, naan or rice.

Egg Curry |  Egg Recipe 

The most readily available ingredient is eggs and it is also the most versatile ingredient. I am going to show you guys, a typical south-indian egg curry that pairs well with steamed rice. 

#EggCurry #EggGravy #EggRecipe #AndhraStyle #IndianRecipe

Prep time: 15 mins
Cook time: 45 mins
Number of servings: 4-5 persons

Ingredients: 

To Make Egg Roast - 

Oil - 2 TSP
Turmeric powder - 1/4 TSP
Chilli Powder - 1/2 TSP
Salt - 1/4 TSP
Eggs - 5 Nos Boiled 

To Make Egg Curry/Gravy -

Oil - 2TBSP
Mustard Seeds - 1/2 TSP
Fenugreek Seeds - 1/4 TSP
Cumin Seeds - 1/4 TSP
Onions - 2 Nos Finely Chopped 
Green chilies - 3 Nos Sliced 
Garlic Cloves 
Curry Leaves 
Tomatoes - 2 Nos Chopped
Ginger Garlic Paste - 1/2 TSP
Turmeric powder - 1/4 TSP
Chilli Powder - 2 TSP
Coriander powder - 1 TSP 
Rock Salt - 1 TSP
Tamarind Puree - 1 CUP Diluted
Water - 2 CUPS 
Roasted Eggs - 5 Nos
Coriander Leaves Chopped 

You can buy our book and classes on http://www.21frames.in/shop

Method

Heat a pan with some oil 
Add red chili powder, salt, turmeric powder and mix well 
Make slits in the boiled eggs and add them to the pan 
Roast the eggs and keep them aside 
Heat a pan with some oil, add onions, green chilies, a few garlic cloves, curry leaves and saute them 
Add ginger garlic paste and saute along 
Add tomatoes and cook them 
Add red chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, salt and mix well 
Add a cup of tamarind puree and  2 cups water and close and cook for 10 mins 
Add the roasted eggs and close and cook for 10 mins 
Finally garnish it with coriander leaves and serve it hot with steamed rice

title: 1 ALL PURPOSE BASE GRAVY MASALA Make Several Curry Recipes
description:
How Make A Base Gravy Masala - Base Gravy Recipe
🔔Please click on the link below and subscribe to Curries With Bumbi so that I can bring more new recipes for you- https://www.youtube.com/@UCc5O8GaMvAGjmvENEvvqZrg 
Ingredients Required For Making Base Gravy Recipe -
*570 gm/4 large onions
* 675 gm/ 6 large tomatoes
* 40 gm/ 3 inch length/ 1 tablespoon grated ginger
* 40 gm/ 12 large garlic cloves or about 1 & 1/2 tablespoon finely grated
* 1 green chilli 
* 30 gm/ 15 cashew nuts soaked in 1/3 rd cup hot water
* 1 & 1/2 teaspoons cumin seeds
* 2 -3 dry bay leaves
* 2 inch length cinnamon stick
* 5 to 6 green cardamoms
* 1 & 1/2 teaspoons salt or to taste
* 1/2 cup oil
For spice paste
* 1 tsp turmeric powder
* 1 tsp cumin powder
* 1 tablespoon Kashmiri Chili powder (please adjust proportion according to your preference)
* 1 tablespoon coriander powder
* 1 tablespoon of garam masala powder
* 1/3 rd cup water
METHOD -
* In a bowl add turmeric powder, cumin powder, Kashmiri chili powder, coriander powder and garam masala powder. Add about a third of a cup of water, mix 
* Heat oil over medium heat
* add dry bay leaves, cinnamon stick, green cardamoms and cumin seeds. Allow the seeds to splutter to release their flavor and then add chopped onions along with a fourth of a teaspoon of salt. Stir the onions on medium heat till they turn slightly brown in color. 
* Add the spice paste and fry on low heat for 2 to 3 mins
* Add chopped green chilli, finely grated ginger and garlic. Fry on medium heat for a few minutes until you see the oil floating on top.
* add crushed tomatoes along with half a teaspoon of salt. Stir on medium heat from time to time till the tomatoes get well fried and cooked and again the oil starts leaving the sides. 
* make a smooth cashew nut paste in your blender for which soak some cashew nut in hot water for about an hour 
*Add the cashew nut paste only after you see the tomatoes and onions have got well cooked and mixed together to become one single mass
* Once again when you start seeing that oil oozing out along the sides or floating on top your base gravy is done. 
* You can store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for about a week or you can divide them into portions and it stores well in the freezer for 6 months.

title: Egg Thokku | How To Make Muttai Thokku | South Indian Style Egg Curry | Egg Recipe By Varun
description:
Egg Thokku Recipe | Muttai Thokku Recipe | Egg Recipe | Anda Recipe | Egg Curry | Egg Masala | Egg Gravy | முட்டை தொக்கு | Get Curried

Learn how to make Egg Thokku recipe with our chef Varun Inamdar.

Eggs always taste good when combined with Indian Spices. Egg Thokku is popular south Indian recipe also know as Mutttai Thokku. Egg Thokku is an easy side dish that serves with chapati and rice. 

Hard Boil Egg - https://youtu.be/5Ca3X_YiWbk

Ingredients:
(Serve 2)
- 4 tbsp Oil
- 2 tsp Fennel Seeds
- 1 Cinnamon Stick
- 1 tbsp Green Chillies
- Curry Leaves
- 1/ 2 cup Onion (sliced)
- 1 tbsp Ginger Garlic Paste
- 1 tsp Turmeric Powder
- 1 tsp Red Chilli Powder
- 1 tsp Garam Masala Powder
- 1/2 cup Tomato Puree
- Salt
- 4 Egg (Boiled)

Method-

For Masala:
1. Heat a pan on high flame, add oil, fennel seeds, cinnamon stick, green chillies, curry leaves, sliced onion and toss it.
2. Let the onion turn golden brown in colour, then add ginger garlic paste and mix it.
3. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder and garam masala powder. Mix it well on high flame.
4. Now add tomato puree, mix this well and sprinkle some salt.

5. Finally cut the egg, add into masala and give a quick toss.

Now your Egg Thokku recipe is ready to be served.

To Make Egg Roast

Egg - 5 Nos
Oil (Buy: https://amzn.to/2RGYvrw)
Turmeric Powder - 1/4 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2RC4fm4)
Red Chilli Powder - 1/2 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/37DAVT1)
Salt - 1/4 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2vg124l) 


To Make Dhaba Style Egg Curry

Oil - 2 Tbsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2RGYvrw)
Whole Spices 
(Bay Leaf, Cinnamon, Cloves, Cardamom)
Chopped Onion - 4 Nos
Ginger Garlic Paste - 1 1/2 (Buy: https://amzn.to/314FymX)
Turmeric Powder - 1/4 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2RC4fm4)
Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder - 2 Tbsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2GgLsrp)
Coriander Powder - 2 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/36nEgEq)
Cumin Powder - 1 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2TPuOXW)
Salt - 1 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2vg124l) 
Slit Green Chilli - 5 Nos
Tomato - 4 Nos
Water - 1 Cup
Garam Masala Powder - 1 Tsp (Buy: https://amzn.to/2TPe8jd)
Kasuri Methi (Buy: https://amzn.to/2TRtrYS)


Method:
Step 1: To roast the eggs
1. Boil the egg in water for 15 mins. 
2. After 15 mins, remove the egg from water, de-sheel and keep it aside. 
3. To a pan, add oil, turmeric powder, chilli powder and salt. 
4. Keep the flame on low and mix the masala powders. 
5. Make small slits on the egg and place them on the pan.
6. Shake the pan and roast the eggs for 2 mins. Keep it aside. 

Step 2: To make the curry
7. To a pan, add oil, Bay Leaf, Cinnamon, Cloves, Cardamom,  onions and saute. 
8. Once the onion turns golden brown color, add the ginger garlic paste.
9. Saute until the raw smell leave. 
10. Add turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, salt and mix well.
11. Add the slit green chillies. Saute everything on medium flame. 
12. Add the tomatoes to a mixer jar and make it into puree. 
13. Pour in the tomato puree and mix everything. 
14. Thin the masala with water and let it simmer for 15 mins.
15. Add garam masala and cook until oil separates. 
16. Thin it a little bit with 1/2 cup of water. 
17. Add the roasted eggs and crushed kasuri methi. Cook for 2 more mins.
18. Dhaba Style Egg Curry is ready to be served. 
19. This tasty curry can be served with chapati, roti, naan and even rice.

Here is the link to Amazon HomeCooking Store where I have curated products that I use and are similar to what I use for your reference and purchase https://www.amazon.in/shop/homecookingshow

முட்டை தக்காளி தொக்கு 
தேவையான பொருட்கள்

முட்டை - 6
எண்ணெய் - 4 டேபிள்ஸ்பூன்
மசாலா பொருட்கள் - (பட்டை, 4 ஏலக்காய், 4 கிராம்பு, சீரகம்)
வெங்காயம் - 3 (நறுக்கியது)
பச்சை மிளகாய் - 4 (நடுவில் கீறியது)
மிளகுத்தூள் - 1 டீஸ்பூன்
இஞ்சி பூண்டு பேஸ்ட் - 1 டீஸ்பூன்
தக்காளி ப்யூரி - 1 (ஒரு தக்காளியைக் குழைத்து தயாரிக்கவும்)
தக்காளி - 3 (நறுக்கியது)
மஞ்சள்தூள் - 1/2 டீஸ்பூன்
காஷ்மீரி மிளகாய்தூள் - 2 டீஸ்பூன்
கொத்தமல்லித்தூள் - 2 டீஸ்பூன்
உப்பு - 1 டீஸ்பூன்
கறிவேப்பிலை - சிறிதளவு
கரம் மசாலா - 1 டீஸ்பூன்
தண்ணீர் - தேவையான அளவு
கொத்தமல்லி - அலங்காரத்திற்கு

செய்முறை:
ஒரு கடாயில் 4 டேபிள்ஸ்பூன் எண்ணெய் சூடாக்கி, பட்டை, ஏலக்காய், கிராம்பு, சீரகம் சேர்த்து நறுமணம் வரும் வரை வதக்கவும்.
நறுக்கிய வெங்காயம், பச்சை மிளகாய் சேர்த்து பொன்னிறமாக வதக்கவும்.
மிளகுத்தூள், இஞ்சி-பூண்டு பேஸ்ட் சேர்த்து நன்கு கிளறவும். தக்காளி ப்யூரி சேர்த்து கிளறி, பின் நறுக்கிய தக்காளியை சேர்த்து மொத்தமாக கலந்து விடவும்..
மஞ்சள்தூள், காஷ்மீரி மிளகாய்தூள், கொத்தமல்லித்தூள், உப்பு சேர்த்து, கறிவேப்பிலையுடன் கலந்து விடவும்.
1/2 கப் தண்ணீர் சேர்த்து மூடி வைத்து 5 நிமிடம் வேகவிடவும்.
வேகவைத்த முட்டைகளில் சிறிய கீறல் போட்டு, கறியுடன் நன்கு புரட்டி 5 நிமிடம் சிம்மில் வைத்துக்கொள்ளவும்.
கடைசியாக, கரம் மசாலா தூவி, கொத்தமல்லி தூவி அலங்கரிக்கவும்.
சூடாக பரிமாறவும்! இது சாதத்திற்கும், சப்பாத்திக்கும் ஏற்ற கிரேவி!

title: Chettinad Egg Drop Curry | Home Cooking
description:
Egg Curry & Gravy Recipes : https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLFhw-Rie62rSJL8swqM_6emJR3ojgd_vx
▶ Next Recipe: https://youtu.be/eMv0NplQW-0

Chettinad Egg Drop Curry
#eggrecipe #eggcurry
Ingredients

Egg - 6 nos
Gingelly oil/Sesame oil - 2 Tbsp
Bay leaf
Cardamom
Clove
Cinnamon 
Onion - 2 nos finely chopped
Green chili - 2 nos
Few Curry leaves 
Tomato - 2 nos chopped
Turmeric powder - 1/2 tsp
Chili powder - 1 tsp
Coriander powder - 2 tsp
Salt to taste
Water 

For Masala Paste
Oil - 1 tsp 
Fennel seeds - 1 tsp
Poppy seeds/Khus khus - 1/2 tsp
Roasted Chana Dal - 1 Tbsp
Red chili - 8 nos
Grated Coconut - 1/2 cup
Piece of Ginger
Garlic - 4 to 5 cloves

Method:
For Dry roast:
1. Heat pan(Buy : https://goo.gl/X9hRl6 ), add oil, fennel seeds, poppy seeds (Khus Khus), roasted chana dal, red chili, grated coconut, ginger, garlic and roast it for few minutes. Add little water and grind (Buy : https://goo.gl/BPiR5y )  the mixture in to a fine paste, after it comes to a room temperature.
2. Heat a pan, add gingely oil, bay leaf, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, onion, green chili and saute it well.
3. Once the onion turns brown add tomato, curry leaves, turmeric powder, chili powder, coriander powder and salt. Saute till tomato mashed up.
4. Add the ground masala paste.
5. Add water to the masala and close the lid. Let it simmer till the raw smell goes off.
6. Crack the egg and drop in to the curry. Close the lid (Buy : https://goo.gl/HVptwr ) and let it cook for 15 minutes on medium low flame.
7. Garnish it with chopped coriander leaves.
Delicious egg drop curry is ready to serve!

title: Traditional Egg Drop Curry | Easy Coconut Egg Curry Recipe | Perfect with Rice
description:
🍳 Mangalorean Egg Drop Curry | Coastal Comfort Food 🌴
Ingredients
For the Masala
4 Kashmiri red chilies
3 spicy dry red chilies
1 tbsp coriander seeds
1 tsp cumin seeds (jeera)
½ tsp fenugreek seeds (methi)
½ tsp black pepper
1 pinch ajwain
½ tsp oil

For Grinding
1 cup grated coconut
1 small ball tamarind
5–6 garlic cloves
Water as needed

For the Curry
Ground masala paste
1½ cups water
Salt to taste
½ tsp turmeric powder
1 finely chopped onion
4–5 eggs
2 green chilies

Method
Heat oil in a pan and roast the Kashmiri chilies, spicy chilies, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, pepper, and ajwain until aromatic.
Transfer to a mixer jar.
Add grated coconut, tamarind, garlic, and a little water. Grind into a smooth paste.
Transfer the ground masala to a kadai.
Add water, salt, turmeric powder, and chopped onion. Mix well.
Bring the curry to a boil.
Reduce the flame to low and gently crack 4–5 eggs directly into the curry.
Cover and cook on low heat for 10–15 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked.
Add slit green chilies and simmer for another minute.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

If you love the bold flavors of Mangalorean cuisine, this Egg Drop Curry is for you! Made with a rich coconut-based masala, aromatic spices, and eggs dropped directly into the simmering curry, this dish is simple yet incredibly flavorful.

Serve it with hot steamed rice for a comforting meal that brings the taste of coastal Karnataka right to your plate.

✨ Authentic Mangalorean flavors ✨ Coconut-based curry ✨ Easy & delicious ✨ Perfect with rice

Don't forget to Like, Share & Subscribe for more Mangalorean and South Indian recipes!

title: Satin Soft Gravy Wali CREAMY GOLDEN SHAHI EGG KORMA | Issey Mazedar EGG CURRY Nahi Khayi Hogi Kabhi
description:
#creamyeggcurry #eggcurry #andacurry #goldeneggkorma #afghaniandakorma #afghanieggcurry #eggkormarecipe  #eggkorma #andakorma #eggrecipes 
egg korma, egg masala, egg curry, dhaba style egg korma, dhaba style anda masala recipe, cook with fem egg masala, egg gravy, anda curry, simple egg curry recipe, egg recipes, how to make egg masala, how to make eggs, homemade egg masala, homemade anda masala, cook with fem recipes, cook with fem egg korma, hyderabadi recipes, egg masala recipe in hindi, egg korma recipe in hindi, egg masala gravy, ande ka salan, malai anda, malai masala

Creamy Golden Egg Curry (Soft Gravy) – English Translation
Ingredients
For the Green Masala Paste
1–2 tbsp oil
2 medium onions (about 150 g), chopped
12 garlic cloves
2-inch piece ginger, chopped
4–5 green chilies, chopped
12 cashew nuts
A little water (for cooking and blending)
2 handfuls fresh coriander (cilantro), including the tender stems
For Frying the Eggs
7–8 boiled eggs
1 tsp red chili powder
1 tsp turmeric powder
1 tsp salt
1 tbsp oil
For the Curry
5–6 tbsp oil
1 tbsp butter
2 green cardamoms
4 cloves
4 small pieces cinnamon
Prepared green masala paste
½ tsp black pepper powder
1 tsp cumin powder
1 tsp garam masala
A pinch of turmeric
2–3 pinches salt (adjust to taste)
4–5 tbsp yogurt (curd)
2–3 tbsp fresh cream (or blended milk cream/malai)
1 tbsp roasted and crushed dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi)
Fresh coriander leaves for garnish
Method
Step 1: Make the Green Masala Paste
Heat 1–2 tablespoons of oil in a pan over high heat.
Add the chopped onions and sauté lightly.
Add the garlic and ginger. Sauté well.
Add the chopped green chilies and cashews.
Continue cooking until the raw smell of the onions disappears.
Add a little water, mix, cover, and cook on low heat for 3–4 minutes.
Turn off the heat and add a generous amount of fresh coriander leaves along with the tender stems.
Let the mixture cool, then blend into a smooth paste, adding a little water if needed.
Step 2: Fry the Eggs
Make a few shallow cuts on the boiled eggs.
Add the eggs to the same pan.
Sprinkle in the red chili powder, turmeric, salt, and oil.
Mix well so the eggs are evenly coated.
Fry until lightly golden, sprinkling a little water if needed during frying.
Remove the eggs and set aside.
Step 3: Prepare the Curry
Heat 5–6 tablespoons of oil and 1 tablespoon of butter in a pan.
Add the cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon.
Fry until fragrant.
Add the prepared green masala paste and cook over high heat, stirring frequently. (Cover the pan if it splatters.)
After 2–3 minutes, add the black pepper, cumin powder, garam masala, turmeric, and salt.
Continue cooking until the oil begins separating from the masala.
Turn off the heat temporarily and stir in the yogurt.
Mix thoroughly before turning the heat back on to prevent the yogurt from curdling.
Cook until the raw smell of the yogurt disappears.
Add the fresh cream (or blended malai) and cook until the oil separates again.
Add the fried eggs along with any remaining spice mixture from the frying pan.
Mix gently to coat the eggs.
Add the roasted and crushed kasuri methi and some fresh coriander leaves.
Cover and cook on low heat for 4–5 minutes.
Serve

Your creamy, soft, golden egg curry is ready. Turn off the heat and serve hot with naan, roti, paratha, or steamed rice.

title: Egg Curry recipe | अंडा करी | Easy Indian main dish recipes | Chef Ajay Chopra
description:
#eggcurry#ajwainmirchiparatha#chefajaychopra#

One of the best ingredients in non – vegetarian category of food is egg. Infact nowadays many vegetarians have also started relishing eggs in their diet. So today we are going to show you how this simple and innocent ingredient can be extra ordinarily #delicious and can make you hero of your family. This is one ingredient, which almost every family of our country cooks and enjoys in their home. So come, let’s check out this #eggcurry recipe with me, #ChefAjayChopra.

Ingredients:
4-5 Boiled Eggs
2 tbsp Oil
4-5 Cloves
1 Black Cardamom
1-2 Bay Leaves
Few Green Cardamoms
1 small piece of Cinnamon
1 tsp Cumin Seeds
1/2cup chopped onions
2 tsp chopped Garlic
1 tsp chopped Ginger
1 tbsp Ginger – Garlic paste
Water
½ tsp turmeric Powder
1 tsp Coriander Powder
1 tsp red Chilli Powder
1 tsp Garam Masala Powder
1 cup Tomato Puree
1 tbsp chopped Green Chillies
Salt to taste
Handful of Coriander Leaves
1 tsp Kasuri Methi / dried Fenugreek Leaves

Method:First boil the eggs. Now after peeling it prick it with the help of a fork. Now take a pan, put some oil and sauté the eggs till it is golden brown in colour. Take the eggs out of the pan. In the same pan, put the whole spices,. Now put onions, ginger, garlic and paste of Ginger-Garlic and mix everything well. Put some water and now add all the powdered spices. Stir for sometime. Now put the tomato puree, salt green chillies and add some water for the gravy. Let it cook for sometime. Once the gravy is half – cooked, put the fried eggs into it. Combine everything well and cover the lid.  Once it is cooked, put the coriander leaves and dried fenugreek leaves into it. Again cover the lid. Let it cook for few minutes. Your egg delicious egg curry is ready. Serve hot along with steam rice or roti. Enjoy!

இன்று இந்தியன் ரிசிபி தமிழில் முட்டை குர்மா கொண்டு வந்துள்ளோம். முட்டை குர்மா ரெசிபி என்பது வேகவைத்த முட்டைகளின் சுவையான கிரேவி ரெசிபி ஆகும். இந்த முட்டை குர்மா சாதாரண சாதம் அல்லது வெறைட்டி சாதம் மற்றும் எந்த உணவுடனும் சாப்பிட நன்றாக இருக்கும். இந்தியன் ரெசிபி தமிழ் | அபியின் செய்முறை

required things: 
6 boiled eggs 
3 tablespoons oil 
1 onion sliced 
handfull small onion
2 red chillies 
1 tablespoon chopped ginger 
1 tablespoon garlic 
3 medium tomatoes chopped 
Salt - to taste 
3 tablespoons red chilli powder 
1 tablespoon coriander powder  
1/2 tsp turmeric powder 
2 tablespoons coconut 
5 cashews 
2 tablespoons chopped coriander leaves Handfull coriander leaves
1 tspoon cumin 
1/2 Tspoons black pepper 
1 cinnamon stick 
5 cardamom 
4-5 cloves 
Curry leaves 
1 1/2 tablespoons fennel

தேவையான பொருட்கள்:
6 வேகவைத்த முட்டைகள் 
3 தேக்கரண்டி எண்ணெய் 
1 வெங்காயம் வெட்டப்பட்டது கைப்பிடி அளவு சின்ன வெங்காயம்
2 சிவப்பு மிளகாய் 
1 தேக்கரண்டி முழு இஞ்சி 
1 தேக்கரண்டி பூண்டு 
3 நடுத்தர  தக்காளி நறுக்கியது 
உப்பு - சுவைக்கு ஏற்ப 
3 தேக்கரண்டி சிவப்பு மிளகாய் தூள் 1 தேக்கரண்டி மல்லி தூள் 
½ தேக்கரண்டி மஞ்சள் தூள் 
2 தேக்கரண்டி தேங்காய் 
5 முந்திரி 
2 தேக்கரண்டி நறுக்கப்பட்ட கொத்தமல்லி இலைகள் 
கைப்பிடி கொத்தமல்லி இலைகள் 
1 தேக்கரண்டி சீரகம் 
½ தேக்கரண்டி கருப்பு மிளகு 
1 இலவங்கப்பட்டை 
5 ஏலக்காய் 
4-5 கிராம்பு
கறிவேப்பிலை 
1 1/2 தேக்கரண்டி பெருஞ்சீரகம்

Instructions :
First pour the oil into the cooking pot and heat. Add garlic, ginger, small onion and tomato and fry till it turns golden brown. Grind and set aside. Grind coconut, cumin, fennel, cashews, coriander leaves, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon and pepper in a mixing jar. Once Pastes well and set aside. Heat the pan and add the oil. Add cinnamon and fennel seeds. Add onion and tomato, add salt to taste and fry the tomatoes till soft. Add spices. Add the onion and tomato paste. Fry until the oil separates. Add coconut paste, water, mix well and fry till the spices are well cooked. Cook until oil appears on the sides and corners.Add water to adjust gravy. Add the peeled boiled eggs to it. Add some coriander leaves. Put on low heat, cover the lid and cook the egg curry for about 5-7 minutes. Delicious and Tasty Egg Kurma is ready.

முட்டை கறி
தேவையான பொருட்கள்

நல்லெண்ணெய்
மஞ்சள் தூள் - 1/2 தேக்கரண்டி
மிளகாய் தூள்
மிளகு தூள்
உப்பு
முட்டை
கடுகு - 1/2 தேக்கரண்டி
சீரகம் - 1 தேக்கரண்டி
வெங்காயம் - 3 நறுக்கியது
பச்சை மிளகாய் - 4 நறுக்கியது
இஞ்சி பூண்டு விழுது - 2 தேக்கரண்டி
தக்காளி விழுது - 3 பழம்
தனியா தூள் - 1 1/2 தேக்கரண்டி
தண்ணீர் - 1 1/2 கப்
வறுத்த முட்டை 

செய்முறை:
1. முட்டையை வறுக்க ஒரு பானில் நல்லெண்ணெய், மஞ்சள் தூள், மிளகாய் தூள், மிளகு தூள், உப்பு சேர்த்து கலந்து விடவும்.
2. அடுத்து நறுக்கிய முட்டையை சேர்த்து இரண்டு பக்கமும் 3 நிமிடம் வறுக்கவும். 
3. குழம்பு செய்ய ஒரு அகல கடாயில் நல்லெண்ணெய் சேர்த்து சூடானதும் கடுகு, சீரகம் சேர்க்கவும்.
4. கடுகு பொரித்தும் வெங்காயம் சேர்த்து பொன்னிறமாகும் வரை வதக்கவும்.
5. பின்பு பச்சை மிளகாய், இஞ்சி பூண்டு விழுது அடுத்தடுத்து சேர்த்து கலந்து விடவும்.
6. தக்காளி விழுது சேர்த்து பச்சை வாசனை போகும் வரை வதக்கவும்.
7. பிறகு மிளகாய் தூள், தனியா தூள், உப்பு, மிளகு தூள் சேர்த்து சேர்த்து கலந்து விடவும்.
8. தண்ணீர் சேர்த்து 5 நிமிடம் கொதிக்க விடவும்.
9. அடுத்து வறுத்த முட்டையை சேர்த்து 5 நிமிடம் கொதிக்க விடவும்.
10. இறுதியாக கறிவேப்பிலை, கொத்தமல்லி இலை சேர்த்து இறக்கவும்.
11. சுவையான முட்டை கறி தயார்.

Egg curry/Muttai curry is a favorite dish for many people. It can be done in many ways. In this video, you can see a very easy gravy based muttai curry which can be done with regular ingredients available in our kitchens all the time. You can enjoy this for rice/roti. It can be served at lunches or dinners. You can also take this for lunch boxes. Do try this recipe and enjoy.

title: SECRETS To Cooking A PERFECT Indian Style CHICKEN CURRY (STEP BY STEP INSTRUCTIONS)
description:
How To Cook Indian Style Chicken Curry - Chicken Curry Recipe For Beginners 
If you follow this video step by step then I assure you that you will be making the best Indian style chicken curry. I tried my best to explain everything in detail and all the tips and tricks possible that go into making chicken curry.

RECIPE HERE - https://bumbicurry.blogspot.com/2025/01/chicken-curry-indian-style.html

Ingredients for making a chicken curry - serves 6 to 7
* 2.2 lb/ 1 kg chicken on bones. Please note that I preferably use chicken on bones for making a chicken curry. It's not that you cannot use boneless chicken but the gravy won't be as tasty. 
* 2 large potatoes quartered or medium potatoes halved. I did not boil the potatoes before adding them. 
* 1 very large onion or 2 medium sized onions. I used quarter of the onion for making a paste.
* 2 inch length ginger
* 10 large garlic cloves
* 1 large tomato
* 2 green chillies (optional)
* 2 large dry bay leaf
* 1 inch length cinnamon stick
* 6 to 7 green cardamoms
* 4 to 5 cloves
* 1/4 th cup plain unflavored yogurt
* 1 teaspoon turmeric powder
* 2 heaped teaspoons Kashmiri red chilli powder. You can even use regular red chilli powder but in that case please use less. Adjust the proportion of any chilli powder according to your tolerance of heat level. Add only half a teaspoon to start with, do a taste test at the end and add more if you feel you want more chilli heat.
* 1 tablespoon ground coriander
* 1 teaspoon garam masala powder.If you want to make garam masala powder at home then here is the recipe - https://youtu.be/ciR43perZbU
* 1/4 th teaspoon ground mace (optional)
* 1/4 th teaspoon ground nutmeg (optional)
* 2 teaspoons salt (add less to start with, you can always add later)
* 1 teaspoon sugar(optional)
* 1 cup hot water. You can go up to a total of 1 & 1/2 cups of hot water if you want more gravy.
* 1/4th cup oil ( 4 to 5 tablespoons) 
Serve this with rice/roti/paratha/naan.

title: DHABA STYLE EGG CURRY | Egg masala gravy | Egg masala | anda masala,
description:
Egg Masala Curry | Egg Masala Gravy | Egg Curry | Egg Masala 
#dhabastyleeggcurry #eggmasala #eggcurry #egggravy #eggmaslagravy #eggmasalacurry #andacurry #andamasala 
#hotelstyleeggcurry 






A perfect Recipe of Egg Curry made like Restaurant/Dhaba style.
Easy Method.
Simple ingredients.

Egg Curry/ Masala Recipe.

Ingredients

3 Boiled & peeled Eggs,
2 medium Onions chopped,
2 medium Tomatoes chopped,
Coriander (Dhania) for granish,
Curry Leaves for tempering,
Ground masala-
2 inch ginger roughly diced,
2 inch Dried Coconut cut into midium slices,
1 1/2 Tbsp White Sesame seeds,
9-10 Garlic cloves.

Method.

Grind - Sesame seeds, dried coconut, Garlic cloves & Ginger, 
1) 1st grind all the ingredients without water,
2) add 1 tbsp of water, 2 tsp of Red chilli pwd,  1 tsp Turmeric     pwd,  2 tsp Garam Masala pwd,
3) Grind to a smooth paste,
4) Add a table spoon of water (If required),
5) grind untill you get a creamy texture(5-6 minutes),
6) On a medium flame add 1/2 cup oil (Groundnut oil),
7) Fry 3 Boiled eggs on a medium low heat, 
8) After 5-6 minutes eggs are golden in color,
9) Transfer all 3 eggs in a bowl,
10) In the same pan add curry leaves (10-12)
11) Add chopped onions,
12) Keep stirring continuously until golden brown,
13) Add chopped tomatoes,
14) Now put the ground masala,
15) Add 1/2 cup of water for the masala to cook,
16) Keep stiring,
17) Add salt to taste,
18) Once the oil starts seperating from masala it means it is ready,
19) In a serving bowl put 2 fried eggs,
20) and transfer hot masala into it,
21) Cut the 3rd egg into half & place it on top of masala,
22) Garnish with Coriander leaves & Serve hot!
23) Egg Masala goes well with Cumin rice, Parathas or Roties.

Ingredients 

To Make the Masala Paste
Oil - 1 Tsp 
Cinnamon
Star Anise
Cardamom
Cloves
Cumin Seeds - 1 Tsp
Fennel Seeds - 1 Tsp
Peppercorns - 1 Tsp
Coriander Seeds - 2 Tsp 
Red Chili - 5 Nos 
Garlic
Ginger 
Grated Coconut - 1/4 Cup
Water

To Roast the Eggs
Oil -1 Tsp 
Turmeric Powder -1/4 Tsp 
Salt - 1/2 Tsp 
Chili Powder - 1 Tsp 
Boiled Eggs - 5 Nos

To Make Chettinad Egg Curry 
Roasted Eggs - 5 Nos
Gingelly Oil - 3 Tbsp 
Onion - 3 Nos Finely Chopped
Few Curry Leaves
Tomatoes - 3 Nos Finely Chopped
Rock Salt - 1 1/2 Tsp 
Ground Masala Paste
Water - 2 Cups

Method

Heat a pan with some oil
Add turmeric powder, chili powder, salt and mix it well
Make slits on the hard boiled eggs and add it to the pan
Roast the eggs and keep it aside
Heat a pan with some oil
Add the ingredients for the masala paste and roast them
Cool them and grind them to a fine paste
Heat a pan with some oil, add onions, curry leaves and saute them till they are golden
Add the tomatoes and saute
Add salt and the ground masala paste
Saute for 5 mins 
Add water and mix well
Let it boil for 5 mins 
Add eggs and mix well
Close and boil it for 5 mins
Serve the Chettinad Egg Curry hot with some rice

title: Easy Egg Curry Recipe | Everyday Indian Cooking | Chef Vicky Ratnani
description:
This Special Egg Curry is all about comfort, balance, and honest flavours.
No unnecessary gimmicks — just a well-made curry that delivers every single time.

I’ve cooked this egg curry using Emami Healthy & Tasty Kacchi Ghani Mustard Oil, which adds a beautiful depth and that unmistakable desi aroma to the dish.

Ingredients:

Emami Kachchi Ghani Strong Mustard Oil
3-4 | Eggs
1 tsp | Mustard seeds (Rai)
1 tsp | Cumin seeds (Jeera)
5-6 | Black peppercorns
1 tsp | Carom seeds (Ajwain)
1 | Medium-sized onion (finely chopped)
1 tbsp | Garlic paste
1 tsp | Dried fenugreek leaves (Kasuri methi)
2 tsp | Kasundi mustard
1-2 tbsp | Tomato purée
Salt (as per taste)
1/2 cup | Coconut milk
1 tsp | Lemon juice
Fresh coriander leaves

Method:
Step 1:
Puncture the eggs lightly with a skewer or toothpick and fry in some hot mustard oil.

Step 2:
Remove the eggs and add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, and carom seeds. 

Step 3:
Add the chopped onion and fry till golden brown.

Step 4:
Incorporate the garlic paste, dried fenugreek leaves, and kasundi mustard.

Step 5:
Pour in some water and let this cook for 3-4 minutes.

Step 6:
Now stir in the tomato purée until well incorporated and add some salt.

Step 7:
Pour in some more water and let this emulsify.

Step 8:
Add the eggs and the coconut milk and stir well.

Step 9:
Top it up with some lemon juice and fresh coriander leaves.

Serving Suggestion:
Garnish with deep-fried curry leaves and fresh coriander, and enjoy this delicious restaurant-style egg curry while it’s still hot!


Whether you’re cooking for family or just craving something familiar, this is one recipe you’ll come back to again and again.

If you love simple Indian food done right, this one’s for you.

title: కుక్కర్లో ఓ అద్భుతం ఈ మసాలా ఎగ్ బిర్యానీ | Easy & Simple Spicy Masala egg biryani recipe in cooker
description:
కుక్కర్లో ఓ అద్భుతం ఈ మసాలా ఎగ్ బిర్యానీ | Easy & Simple Spicy Masala egg biryani recipe in cooker | egg biryani recipe | One pot egg biryani | egg biryani in cooker || Masala egg biryani recipe in telugu  @VismaiFood 

Hello Foodies!!! 

Today We are sharing the simple yet super tasty Masala Egg Biryani in pressure cooker recipe. This Egg Biryani recipe is so simple to make with limited ingredients and steps , that even the bachelor can prepare so easily in cooker itself. preparation method involves soaking of rice then frying of onions, followed by frying of boiled eggs. For gravy fry tomatoes, masalas , ginger garlic paste and finally add diluted full cream curd along with water and finally to make the yummy tasty Masala Egg biryani add soaked rice sand boiled fried eggs to gravy and keep the cooker on normal flame for 2 whistles to get the amazing quick Egg Biryani. This can be best served with chill raitha. Hope you try this tasty biryani at your home and enjoy, if so please do share those recipe pics to our Instagram handle.

title: Muttai Kulambu in Tamil | Egg Gravy in Tamil | Egg Curry in Tamil
description:
In this video we will see how to make Muttai Kulambu in Tamil. Muttai Kulambu is an ideal combination for rice and for chapathi. Well seasoned boiled eggs along with delicious gravy makes it an excellent choice for serving it as a gravy / side dish. In this recipe we have made egg curry with coconut because coconut adds thickness and creaminess to our kuzhambu. But if you don't like the taste of coconut you can definitely make this recipe without coconut. The consistency of the kulambu can be adjusted depending on what it ti going to be served with.

Friends, please do make this simple and easy egg gravy and serve it with hot chapathi / rice. Also please do share your feedback about this recipe in the comments below. All the best and happy cooking !

For detailed Muttai Kulambu Recipe / Egg gravy recipe please visit
